content.text:
Now I have a clear picture. Let me look at what's wrong with the existing tests and then write proper ones for auth + favorites.

**Issues with `search.test.js`:**
- No `describe` groups — flat test list is hard to scan
- `vi.stubGlobal` is called inside tests and never restored → mock state can leak
- Test 2 mocks a wrong response shape (`data` is an object, not an array) but still passes — it only checks status 200, not the body
- No assertions on response body content in the happy path

The new tests will demonstrate the correct patterns. They assume this file structure you'll implement:
- `src/db.js` — exports `{ query }` (pg pool wrapper)
- `src/middleware/auth.js` — exports `{ requireAuth }`
- Routes added to `router.js`: `POST /api/auth/register`, `POST /api/auth/login`, `GET|POST|DELETE /api/favorites`

content.text:
Both files are written. Here's a summary of the key patterns and why they matter:

---

### What changed vs the existing `search.test.js`

| Old pattern | New pattern | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Flat `test()` calls | `describe` blocks | Groups related tests; easier to see what feature is failing |
| `vi.stubGlobal` inside tests, never restored | `vi.mock()` at module level + `vi.clearAllMocks()` in `beforeEach` | `vi.mock()` is permanent for the file; `clearAllMocks` resets state between tests instead of leaking it |
| Only checks status code | Asserts on `res.body` shape and specific fields | Catches bugs where a route returns 200 with the wrong data |
| No sad-path tests | Validates empty inputs, wrong passwords, DB errors, missing auth | Most bugs live in the edge cases |

---

### Key concepts demonstrated

**`vi.mock()` hoisting** — Vitest transforms `vi.mock()` to run before imports, so `query` in `auth.test.js` is already the mock function by the time any test runs.

**`mockResolvedValueOnce` chaining** — lets you script multiple DB calls in a single test (e.g. the "check username, then INSERT" flow in register).

**Auth middleware as a configurable mock** — `asUser()` / `asGuest()` helpers make the intent of each test explicit in one line, instead of hiding auth setup inside the test body.

**`WHERE id = $1 AND user_id = $2`** — the DELETE 404 test encodes the security rule: a user can't delete someone else's favorites. The query returning no rows handles both cases.

---

These tests will fail (red) until you implement the routes — that's the point. Write just enough code to make each test pass, one at a time.

46	## Tech Constraints
47	
48	- **No async/await** — `.then()` / `.catch()` Promise chains only
49	- **No frontend framework** — vanilla HTML, CSS, JS
50	- **No build tools** — files served as static assets
51	
52	## Architecture
53	
54	**Proxy pattern**: frontend never calls Jikan directly. All API calls go through the Express server.
55	
56	| Express route            | Jikan endpoint    |
57	| ------------------------ | ----------------- |
58	| `/api/top-anime`         | `GET /v4/top/anime` |
59	| `/api/search?q=...`      | `GET /v4/anime?q={query}` |
60	| `/api/anime-details/:id` | `GET /v4/anime/{id}` |
61	
62	- Routes use kebab-case
63	- Detail page URL: `/anime/:id` — Express serves `public/detail/index.html` via manual route in `router.js`
64	- Frontend reads anime ID from `window.location.pathname.split("/")[2]`
65	- Cards on home and search pages navigate to `/anime/{mal_id}`
66	- URL encoding via `encodeURIComponent` on search and detail pages
67	
68	## Jikan API
69

71	
72	Response shape:
73	- Top-level keys: `pagination`, `data`
74	- Anime array at `response.data`
75	- Each anime: `title`, `score`, `mal_id`, `images.jpg.image_url`
76	
77	## Tests
78	
79	- Framework: Vitest + supertest
80	- Global setup: `src/tests/setup.js` (configured in `vitest.config.js`)
81	- Stubs in `src/tests/stubs/` (real Jikan response shapes)
82	- Test names describe user behavior, not implementation
83	- Each test validates: status code, content type, response body shape
84	
85	### Database in tests
86	
87	- Each test gets a fresh in-memory PostgreSQL via [PGlite](https://pglite.dev/)
88	- `src/tests/setup.js` handles `beforeEach`/`afterEach`: creates PGlite instance, runs `src/schema.sql`, seeds a test user (`testuser` / `hashed_password_123`), and swaps `db.sql` to a PGlite-backed wrapper
89	- Controllers use `db.sql` tagged template — same interface in prod (postgres.js) and test (PGlite wrapper)
90	- **Never mock `db.sql`** — queries run against real SQL in PGlite
91	- To add tables: update `src/schema.sql`, they'll be created automatically each test
92	
93	### Mocking external APIs
94	
95	- **Only mock external HTTP calls** (Jikan API), never database calls
96	- Mock pattern: `vi.stubGlobal("fetch", () => Promise.resolve({ ok: true, json: () => Promise.resolve(stub) }))`
97	- Import stubs: `import stub from "./stubs/search-one-piece.json" with { type: "json" }`
98	- No external API calls in tests — always mock fetch
99	
100	## Remaining Work
101	
102	- [ ] Caching on Express routes (avoid Jikan rate limits)
103	- [ ] Unhappy path tests (rate limit simulation, invalid ID)
104	- [ ] CSS styling for all 3 pages

file.content:
# Project: Anime Showcase & Search

Express server serving 3 static HTML/JS/CSS pages, proxying data from the [Jikan API](https://docs.api.jikan.moe/).

## Pages & Routes

| Route        | File                       | Purpose           |
| ------------ | -------------------------- | ----------------- |
| `/`          | `public/index.html`        | Top anime home    |
| `/search/`   | `public/search/index.html` | Search by name    |
| `/anime/:id` | `public/detail/index.html` | Anime detail view |

## File Structure

```
public/
  index.html        ← top anime home page
  app.js
  style.css
  search/
    index.html
    app.js
    style.css
  detail/
    index.html
    app.js
    style.css
src/
  app.js            ← Express app, serves public/ via express.static
  server.js         ← starts server on port 9080
  router.js         ← API routes + manual route for /anime/:id
  db.js             ← exports db.sql tagged template (postgres.js in prod, PGlite in test)
  schema.sql        ← database schema (users table)
  controllers/
    top-anime.js
    anime-search.js
    anime-details.js
  tests/
    setup.js        ← global test setup (PGlite per test, schema, seed)
    search.test.js
    db.test.js
    stubs/
      search-one-piece.json
vitest.config.js    ← wires setup.js for all tests
```

## Tech Constraints

- **No async/await** — `.then()` / `.catch()` Promise chains only
- **No frontend framework** — vanilla HTML, CSS, JS
- **No build tools** — files served as static assets

## Architecture

**Proxy pattern**: frontend never calls Jikan directly. All API calls go through the Express server.

| Express route            | Jikan endpoint    |
| ------------------------ | ----------------- |
| `/api/top-anime`         | `GET /v4/top/anime` |
| `/api/search?q=...`      | `GET /v4/anime?q={query}` |
| `/api/anime-details/:id` | `GET /v4/anime/{id}` |

- Routes use kebab-case
- Detail page URL: `/anime/:id` — Express serves `public/detail/index.html` via manual route in `router.js`
- Frontend reads anime ID from `window.location.pathname.split("/")[2]`
- Cards on home and search pages navigate to `/anime/{mal_id}`
- URL encoding via `encodeURIComponent` on search and detail pages

## Jikan API

Base URL: `https://api.jikan.moe/v4`

Response shape:
- Top-level keys: `pagination`, `data`
- Anime array at `response.data`
- Each anime: `title`, `score`, `mal_id`, `images.jpg.image_url`

## Tests

- Framework: Vitest + supertest
- Global setup: `src/tests/setup.js` (configured in `vitest.config.js`)
- Stubs in `src/tests/stubs/` (real Jikan response shapes)
- Test names describe user behavior, not implementation
- Each test validates: status code, content type, response body shape

### Database in tests

- Each test gets a fresh in-memory PostgreSQL via [PGlite](https://pglite.dev/)
- `src/tests/setup.js` handles `beforeEach`/`afterEach`: creates PGlite instance, runs `src/schema.sql`, seeds a test user (`testuser` / `hashed_password_123`), and swaps `db.sql` to a PGlite-backed wrapper
- Controllers use `db.sql` tagged template — same interface in prod (postgres.js) and test (PGlite wrapper)
- **Never mock `db.sql`** — queries run against real SQL in PGlite
- To add tables: update `src/schema.sql`, they'll be created automatically each test

### Mocking external APIs

- **Only mock external HTTP calls** (Jikan API), never database calls
- Mock pattern: `vi.stubGlobal("fetch", () => Promise.resolve({ ok: true, json: () => Promise.resolve(stub) }))`
- Import stubs: `import stub from "./stubs/search-one-piece.json" with { type: "json" }`
- No external API calls in tests — always mock fetch

## Remaining Work

- [ ] Caching on Express routes (avoid Jikan rate limits)
- [ ] Unhappy path tests (rate limit simulation, invalid ID)
- [ ] CSS styling for all 3 pages

content.text:
The approach is fundamentally different. Key rules I missed:
- **Never mock `db.sql`** — PGlite handles a real in-memory database per test
- The seeded `testuser / hashed_password_123` is available in every test
- For favorites: login as `testuser` in `beforeEach` to get a real session cookie instead of mocking the middleware

The existing `auth.test.js` and `favorites.test.js` I wrote use `vi.mock("../db.js")` which directly violates the new guidance. Let me rewrite both, and add the `db.test.js` the file structure lists.

content.text:
All three test files are now correct. Here's a summary of what changed and why:

---

### What changed from the first version

**The big one: no more `vi.mock("../db.js")`**

The first version faked the database with `mockResolvedValueOnce`. That means a typo in your SQL, a missing column, or a wrong constraint would never be caught — the mock just returns whatever you told it to. PGlite runs your actual SQL, so the tests fail if the schema is wrong.

**`favorites.test.js` — login instead of mocking middleware**

A `beforeEach` logs in as `testuser` and captures the session cookie. Every authenticated request then `.set("Cookie", sessionCookie)`. This tests the full stack: auth middleware + session handling + real DB query.

The "delete" test also demonstrates chaining two requests: POST to create, then DELETE using the `id` from the response — the only way to get a real id without faking the DB.

**`db.test.js` — extended with favorites tests**

Three new cases that can't be caught at the HTTP level:
- Insert + retrieve a favorite
- `SELECT WHERE user_id` only returns the right user's rows
- `DELETE WHERE id AND user_id` won't touch another user's data (the ownership check your SQL must enforce)

husky - pre-commit script failed (code 1)

content.text:
The pre-commit hook runs the full test suite and blocks the commit because the routes don't exist yet — that's expected TDD (red phase). The failures are:

- `auth.test.js` — `/api/auth/register` and `/api/auth/login` return 404 (not implemented)
- `favorites.test.js` — same, plus `sessionCookie` is `undefined` because login has no route yet
- `db.test.js` favorites tests — the `favorites` table isn't in `schema.sql` yet

These tests are *supposed* to fail until you implement the routes. The hook prevents committing failing tests.

Do you want to skip the hook for this commit (`--no-verify`) since this is intentional TDD? Or would you prefer to implement enough to make them pass first?

input.command:
git add src/tests/auth.test.js src/tests/favorites.test.js src/tests/db.test.js && git commit --no-verify -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
tests: add auth, favorites, and db tests using PGlite

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

read search.test.js 
and fix those tests depends on other tests files

content.content: File unchanged since last read. The content from the earlier Read tool_result in this conversation is still current — refer to that instead of re-reading.
File unchanged since last read. The content from the earlier Read tool_result in this conversation is still current — refer to that instead of re-reading.

content.text:
Issues in `search.test.js`:

1. No `describe` block — flat tests, unlike auth/favorites
2. No `afterEach(() => vi.restoreAllMocks())` — `vi.stubGlobal("fetch")` leaks between tests
3. Test at line 10 mocks `data` as an object, not an array (wrong Jikan shape), and only checks status — so the bug is invisible
4. Tests at lines 10 and 27 test the same happy path redundantly — one with a bad mock, one with the real stub
5. Test names don't describe expected behavior

content.content: The file /home/mohmqat/Work/week-5-project-express/src/tests/search.test.js has been updated successfully.

lines: +import { describe, test, expect, vi, afterEach } from "vitest";
+// Only mock external HTTP calls (fetch to Jikan API), never database calls.
-test("search with valid query returns anime results", () => {
+  test("returns 400 when no query is provided", () => {
+  test("returns 200 with an array of anime for a valid query", () => {
+  test("returns 429 when Jikan is rate limited", () => {
-test("search returns 429 when Jikan is rate limited", () => {

content.text:
Changes:
- Added `describe` block + `afterEach(() => vi.restoreAllMocks())` so the stubbed `fetch` doesn't leak
- Removed the duplicate happy-path test that used a wrong mock shape (`data` as an object instead of an array) and had no body assertions
- Kept one happy-path test using the real stub, asserting both content-type and `data` is an array
- Added a body assertion to the 400 test (checks the error message mentions "query")
